headline: Can $3.5B Legal Bottleneck Threaten EchoStar’s $20B SpaceX Lifeline
summary: EchoStar (SATS) has pulled off the ultimate pivot. Since mid-2025, the stock has rocketed from $15 to $127 as investors treat the company as a de facto tracking stock for SpaceX’s looming $2 trillion IPO. The catalyst? A massive $20 billion spectrum deal that swapped EchoStar's debt-ridden telecom business for a seat at Elon Musk’s table.

headline: Nvidia and Its Big Tech Peers Are on a Bad Run. Goldman Sachs Says It’s Time to Buy.
summary: Big Tech stocks have taken a battering lately—but investors should take the opportunity to buy the dip as the Iran war drags on, according to Goldman Sachs.  Tech in 2026 has posted one of its worst periods of relative underperformance of the past 50 years amid a pivot to value stocks, analyst Peter Oppenheimer said in a research note on Tuesday.  Facebook owner  Meta Platforms  IT company  Microsoft  and chip maker  Nvidia  are all fetching less than 20 times expected earnings for the next 24 months, the analyst noted.

headline: Uber bets on Amazon's custom chips to boost AI efforts
summary: Uber is using Amazon's custom chips to ‌speed up computing and ‌train artificial intelligence models, the cloud ​giant said on Tuesday, as the ride-hailing firm seeks advanced hardware to handle growing digital workloads. The ‌deal expands ⁠the companies' existing cloud partnership by enabling Uber ⁠to use Amazon Web Services' Graviton chips to support smoother ​rides and ​deliveries and ​Trainium processors to ‌train AI models that power its apps. Uber is working to optimize its digital interface, accelerate ride-matching and personalize user experiences ‌to attract users ​and gain a ​competitive edge.

content.title: Uber bets on Amazon's custom chips to boost AI efforts
content.summary: Uber is using Amazon's custom chips to ‌speed up computing and ‌train artificial intelligence models, the cloud ​giant said on Tuesday, as the ride-hailing firm seeks advanced hardware to handle growing digital workloads. The ‌deal expands ⁠the companies' existing cloud partnership by enabling Uber ⁠to use Amazon Web Services' Graviton chips to support smoother ​rides and ​deliveries and ​Trainium processors to ‌train AI models that power its apps. Uber is working to optimize its digital interface, accelerate ride-matching and personalize user experiences ‌to attract users ​and gain a ​competitive edge.

headline: Nucleus Research Releases 2026 DSML Technology Value Matrix
summary: In 2026, the data science and machine learning (DSML) platform market is delivering measurable ROI through the rise of agentic AI and the shift toward autonomous analytics execution. Platforms are no longer evaluated solely on model development capabilities. They are increasingly assessed on how effectively they orchestrate data pipelines, model lifecycle management, and agent-driven workflows that accelerate decision-making and reduce manual effort.

content.title: Nucleus Research Releases 2026 DSML Technology Value Matrix
content.summary: In 2026, the data science and machine learning (DSML) platform market is delivering measurable ROI through the rise of agentic AI and the shift toward autonomous analytics execution. They are increasingly assessed on how effectively they orchestrate data pipelines, model lifecycle management, and agent-driven workflows that accelerate decision-making and reduce manual effort.

headline: Hyperscalers Shift To Arm Chips What It Means For AMD Valuation
summary: Major cloud providers are reallocating AI server spending from legacy x86 CPUs by NasdaqGS:AMD and Intel toward proprietary Arm-based chips. Arm CPUs are projected to account for most custom AI ASIC deployments by 2029, signaling a shift in how hyperscalers design their data center infrastructure. This transition affects demand patterns for traditional x86 data center processors used in large scale AI and cloud workloads. For you as an investor looking at NasdaqGS:AMD, this shift comes at a...

headline: USPS to retain 80% of its Amazon deliveries under new deal
summary:
STORY: Amazon said it reached a new agreement with the U.S. Postal Service on package deliveries.

Sources said the deal allows the cash-strapped mail system to retain about 80% of its existing deliveries from Amazon, its biggest customer.

That's a dramatically better outcome for USPS than the two-thirds or larger cut to deliveries Amazon had threatened, according to a Reuters report last month.

USPS had warned that it could run out of cash as soon as October, and the risk that Amazon would replace the carrier by expanding its own delivery network or using rivals was an existential threat.

Sources said Amazon will continue to expand its delivery network, but not to the point of rivaling the U.S. Postal Service.  

USPS didn’t immediately comment on the new agreement.

content.title: USPS to retain 80% of its Amazon deliveries under new deal
content.summary:

USPS didn’t immediately comment on the new agreement.

headline: CrowdStrike Joins Anthropic Project Glasswing As AI Partnership Meets Valuation Questions
summary: CrowdStrike Holdings (NasdaqGS:CRWD) has been selected as a partner in Anthropic's "Project Glasswing". The company gains early access to Anthropic’s Claude Mythos Preview AI model for defensive cybersecurity work. This partnership places CrowdStrike alongside large technology players such as Amazon, Microsoft, and Apple within the project. CrowdStrike focuses on cloud delivered cybersecurity, with a core platform built around endpoint protection, threat intelligence, and security...
